channel.title: Durham Civic Hub - Local Government News
channel.description: Local government news for Durham County, NC: budget, schools, housing, transit, public health.

item.title: NC Central leadership responds after fatal shooting at party attended by students
item.description: Social media posts describing a Zebulon party where five people were shot suggested it was for North Carolina Central University students to celebrate the end of the semester.

item.title: A Durham Death Doula’s Guide for Better Living and Dying
item.description: In a culture that will do anything to avoid talking about mortality, how should we think about death ? In a new book, Jane K. Callahan offers an honest, empowering framework for preparing for the end.

item.title: More Than 50 Years After Zoning Paved the Way for Development of Black Meadow Ridge, Half the Site Is Being Preserved
item.description: The Eno River Association helped spare 27 acres near West Point on the Eno from development. But the question remains: Why wasn’t Black Meadow Ridge protected to begin with?

item.title: County Manager to Present FY2026-27 Budget on May 11
item.description: County Manager Claudia Hager will present the recommended FY2026-27 budget to the Board of Commissioners on May 11. A public hearing is scheduled for May 26 at 7 PM at 200 E. Main St. The Board is expected to adopt the budget at its June 8 meeting.

item.title: Triangle Counties Come Out Against Proposed Cap on Local Property Taxes
item.description: Durham, Orange, and Wake counties warn that a potential constitutional amendment limiting property tax increases would jeopardize critical services.

item.title: Feds Fine Durham-Based Energy Efficiency Company $722 Million
item.description: American Efficient says it was incentivizing energy savings at major companies. One Federal Energy Regulatory Commission member claims “their entire business is a scam.”

item.title: Earth Month Profile: Marc Maximov, Bike Durham
item.description: Longtime cyclist and Bike Durham board member Marc Maximov shares his roadmap for embracing life without a car.

item.title: Budget Public Hearing: Have Your Say on May 26
item.description: Durham County will hold a public hearing on the FY2026-27 budget on May 26 at 7 PM in the Commissioners Chambers at 200 E. Main St. Neighbors can sign up to speak in person or submit written comments to the Board.

item.title: It’s not rocket science: UNC System leaders make the case for increased funding
item.description: There’s perhaps no better advocate for North Carolina’s higher education system these days than NASA Astronaut Christina Koch. A mission specialist on Artemis II, Koch graduated from the North Carolina School of Science and Mathematics in Durham in 1997 and went on to North Carolina State…

item.title: Young cancer survivor breaks ground on family housing for children in treatment at Duke Hospital
item.description: It would be an understatement to say that Harper Harrell has a smile that lights up a room — it lights up all of the outdoors. That was evident this week when the 12-year-old cancer survivor’s smile lit up her Durham backyard as she buzzed about, sharing with all comers her plans to build safe,…

item.title: DPS Board Submits FY2026-27 County Funding Request
item.description: The Durham Public Schools Board of Education has submitted its FY2026-27 county appropriation request to the Durham County Board of Commissioners. The full recommended budget document is posted on the DPS Board of Education page.

item.title: Applications Open for County Advisory Boards and Commissions
item.description: Durham County is accepting applications for vacancies on advisory boards and commissions, including the Planning Commission, Library Advisory Board, and others. Any Durham County neighbor can apply. Appointments are made by the Board of Commissioners.

item.title: Two Durham elementary students detained by ICE, deported to Honduras within 48 hours
item.description: Two Durham elementary school students and their parents were detained by federal immigration authorities during a scheduled appointment in Charlotte earlier this week, and deported to Honduras within 48 hours, according to family members and advocates. The children, Genesis, 11, and Denis, 6,…

item.title: BOCC Budget Work Sessions Begin in April
item.description: The Durham County Board of Commissioners is holding a series of budget work sessions throughout April and May to review departmental requests ahead of the County Manager's recommended budget presentation on May 11. Agendas and video recordings are posted on the BOCC agenda library page.

item.title: GoDurham Fixed-Route Buses Continue Fare-Free Service
item.description: GoDurham fixed-route bus service remains fare-free for all riders. The Bull City Connector downtown circulator also runs free of charge Monday through Saturday. Trip planning tools and schedules are available at godurhamtransit.org.

item.title: Voter Registration Open for November 2026 General Election
item.description: North Carolina voter registration is open year-round for the November 3, 2026 general election. Durham County neighbors can register online, by mail, or in person at the Durham County Board of Elections. Same-day registration is available during early voting.

item.title: Durham County Library: Free Cards, Free Programs, All Branches
item.description: Durham County Library offers free library cards to all Durham County neighbors with proof of address. All branches provide books, e-books, computers, Wi-Fi, and free community programs. No fines for overdue materials at any branch.
